directives.note: Ungrounded check-in process: 'Check-in is inside the lobby, and a teacher will walk you and your kid to the room.' No atom or fact in the allocation describes Paradox's actual check-in workflow or that a teacher escorts parent+child to the ParaTots room. Either route to content collection for the partner's real check-in process or drop the invented logistics — keep only the time/address which are grounded in fact 631e87bd.

Section 2 repeats 'Saturdays at 9:15 AM' across primary_heading ('Saturdays at 9:15 in Redlands') + tagline ('AGES 0 TO 4, SATURDAYS AT 9:15') + body ('Saturdays at 9:15 AM at 51 W Olive') + items[0].item_body ('Saturdays at 9:15 AM at 51 W Olive'). The address ('51 W Olive') also appears 3x in the section. Pick the slot where each fact lands best (tagline for time/age; items[0] for address) and drop the others. Intra-section redundancy — the visitor reads the same logistic 4 times in ~80 words.

Section 3 body repeats Section 2's logistics nearly verbatim ('ParaTots starts Saturdays at 9:15. At 10:00 the whole church gathers at 51 W Olive'). The 9:15 + 10:00 pairing already lives in the heading 'Come at 9:15, Stay for 10:00'. Tighten the body so it carries the invitation, not a third restatement of the time and address.

Verbatim atom 2528ca66 ('BEGINNING WITH A STRONG SENSE OF BELONGING') is placed in the hero 'body' slot rather than a tagline/kicker slot. Casing is preserved correctly, but designer treatment is at risk — the all-caps line will render as a paragraph body, not a styled kicker. Confirm hero_inner has no tagline/kicker slot available; if it does, move it there. Flagged in draft validation already.

problem_lines: Saturdays at 9:15 AM at 51 W Olive, Redlands. Check-in is inside the lobby, and a teacher will walk you and your kid to the room.
ParaTots meets Saturdays at 9:15 AM at 51 W Olive in Redlands, for kids ages 0 to 4.
ParaTots starts Saturdays at 9:15. At 10:00 the whole church gathers at 51 W Olive, kids and parents in the same room.
standout_lines: before you were even born, god loved you first
A quiet room off the gathering space with somewhere to sit, feed a baby, or settle a fussy one. If you need a minute to breathe, take it. Nobody is counting.
Raising kids under five is a mystery, and nobody here pretends otherwise. Kids are loud in the lobby and welcome to be.
Blocks, crayons, and floor time with teachers who know your kid by name. Belonging starts here, before any of it has words.
summary: Tonally this draft is one of the cleanest Paradox pages I've seen — the hero is quiet, section 1 carries delight in the details without performing warmth, and section 4's 'Raising kids under five is a mystery, and nobody here pretends otherwise' lands the solidarity close exactly as outlined. All four verbatim atoms preserved exact. Two real defects gate it from green: (1) Section 2 invents a check-in workflow ('Check-in is inside the lobby, and a teacher will walk you and your kid to the room') — no atom or fact carries this; classic logistic invention that drops claim_plausibility to ~55. (2) Section 2 is internally redundant — Saturdays/9:15/51 W Olive each appear 3-4 times within ~80 words (heading + tagline + body + items[0]), and Section 3's body re-states the same pair a third time. Hero body slot also carries an all-caps atom that's structurally a kicker; designer-treatment risk, drafter already flagged. Recommend minor_edits with a slot_edit pass on section 2 to drop the invented check-in line and de-duplicate the logistics across heading/tagline/body/items.
